channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Meghalaya Cement Companies Transported 2.93 Lakh MT of Coal Without Mandatory Approvals: High Court Panel
item.description: A committee appointed by the Meghalaya High Court has reported serious violations by two cement companies in the state concerning the transport of coal. Between February 2025 and February 2026, these ...

item.title: Two Men, Including Social Activist, Shot Dead in Bajaur
item.description: In Bajaur district of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, two men lost their lives in separate violent incidents on Sunday. The first victim, Malik Ziaur Rehman, a well-known social activist, was attacked near his ho...

item.title: Nigeria leads African Union Peace and Security Council for May 2026
item.description: Nigeria don officially take the leadership of the African Union Peace and Security Council (PSC) for May 2026. This na big step as e mark Nigeria return to the chairmanship of this 15-member council s...

item.title: Kenya Power Schedules Planned Electricity Outages in Four Counties on Monday, May 4
item.description: Nairobi – Kenyans in parts of Nairobi, Kericho, Uasin Gishu, and Kirinyaga counties should expect electricity interruptions on Monday, May 4, due to planned maintenance by the Kenya Power and Lighting...

item.title: Taiwan President Lai Ching-te Visits Eswatini for King's Anniversary Despite China's Objections
item.description: Taiwanese President Lai Ching-te arrived in Eswatini on a surprise trip to mark the 40th anniversary of King Mswati III's accession to the throne. The visit highlights Taiwan's efforts to maintain dip...

item.title: Peter Obi Leaves ADC Citing Political Interference and Internal Challenges
item.description: Peter Obi, former Labour Party presidential candidate, don officially announce say e don leave African Democratic Congress (ADC) wey e join after im waka comot from Labour Party. For one long post for...

item.title: Guidelines for Timing Your Mail-In Vote in the California Primary
item.description: California voters are receiving their mail-in ballots for the June 2 primary, with more than 23 million ballots mailed to active registered voters. The gubernatorial race features a wide field of cand...

item.title: Community Rally Marks One Year Since Jack and Lilly Sullivan Went Missing
item.description: Under a clear sky in central Nova Scotia, around 50 people gathered outside the RCMP detachment in Stellarton to demand answers about the disappearance of siblings Jack and Lilly Sullivan, who went mi...

item.title: Man in Bungoma Buried in Unusual Seated Position in Semi-Closed Coffin
item.description: Kenyans online have been buzzing with debate after a man from Webuye was buried in an unconventional manner. In a video shared on social media, the deceased was laid to rest in a unique cream-coloured...

item.title: Vosloorus shop owner rescued after month-long abduction, family calls for trauma support
item.description: Mazwi Kubheka, a 27-year-old spaza shop owner from Vosloorus, has been safely returned after enduring a 30-day abduction that left him traumatised and in need of psychological support. According to Ga...

item.title: Zack Polanski defends concerns over police conduct following Golders Green attack
item.description: Zack Polanski, leader of the Green Party in England and Wales, has expressed ongoing concern about the conduct of police officers involved in the arrest of a suspect in the Golders Green attack, despi...

item.title: Concerns Mount Over Pentagon Leadership and Recent Military Purges
item.description: Since the return of Donald Trump to the presidency in January last year, Pentagon leadership has faced unprecedented turmoil under Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth. Hegseth, a former Fox News host known...

item.title: Government Charges Drive High Airfare Costs in Nigeria
item.description: Plenty Nigerians don dey complain say to fly for Naija dey very expensive. The International Air Transport Association (IATA) don come confirm say na government taxes and charges dey make ticket dem h...

item.title: Kitui Police Seize Ammunition and Government Property from Passenger Bus
item.description: In a major security operation along the Thika–Garissa Highway at Kanyonyoo, Kitui County, a joint security team intercepted a passenger bus carrying a suspicious consignment. The bus, registered KCW 2...

item.title: High Petrol Prices Force Many Abuja Residents to Use Public Transport
item.description: Plenty car owners for Federal Capital Territory don dey shift from using their personal cars to public transport because petrol price don rise well-well. On top this matter, low-income workers wey no ...

item.title: Heavy Rains Cause Mudslides in Tharaka Nithi, Elgeyo-Marakwet, and Kiambu; 18 Confirmed Dead
item.description: Kenya is currently facing severe weather challenges as heavy rains have caused deadly mudslides in Tharaka Nithi, Elgeyo-Marakwet, and Kiambu counties. The National Police Service (NPS) has confirmed ...

item.title: Victorian winemakers lose $1.8 million in stock after cool store destroyed by bushfires
item.description: A Victorian couple, Tom and Charmaine Handyside, lost their entire wine vintage worth $1.8 million when bushfires destroyed a cool store in Harcourt on 9 January. The couple, who run Still Moon wine, ...

item.title: North-West Governors Abandon Bandit Negotiations, Focus on Security Measures
item.description: After years wey dem dey try negotiate with bandits for North-West Nigeria, the governors don finally yan say dem no go do any more talks. The talks wey dem try before never solve the wahala, as bandit...

item.title: Kaizer Chiefs Suffer Derby Hangover in Bloemfontein Defeat but Maintain Third Place Lead
item.description: Kaizer Chiefs experienced a tough outing in the Betway Premiership after a 2-0 loss to Siwelele FC at the Free State Stadium in Bloemfontein on Wednesday. Coach Cedric Kaze admitted that his players w...

item.title: Sandeep Pathak Reacts to Two FIRs Filed Against Him, Denies Any Knowledge of the Cases
item.description: Sandeep Pathak, the BJP leader and Rajya Sabha MP, addressed the media after reports surfaced about two FIRs being lodged against him. Speaking in Delhi, Pathak clarified that he had not been informed...
